title: NBA Daily Dunk: March 9, 2026
subtitle: Your daily dose of hoops headlines and highlights!

items.headline: Tatum Triumphs! Celtics Star Returns from Achilles Surgery, Drops 20 in Win!
items.summary: Jayson Tatum made a triumphant return to the court, scoring 20 points to lead the Boston Celtics to a 109-98 victory over the Cleveland Cavaliers after recovering from Achilles surgery.

items.headline: Giannis Sidelined Again with Calf Strain; Bucks Face Magic Without MVP
items.summary: Milwaukee Bucks superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo was ruled out of Sunday's game against the Orlando Magic due to a persistent right calf strain, an injury that has already kept him off the court for a significant period this season.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Back in Action, Fuels Blazers' Victory with 18 Points!
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija returned from a six-game absence due to a lower back injury, contributing 18 points and eight assists in the team's dominant 131-111 win over the Indiana Pacers.

items.headline: Lakers Hold Off Knicks Behind Doncic's Dominance
items.summary: Luka Doncic poured in 35 points as the Los Angeles Lakers secured a 110-97 victory over the New York Knicks.

items.headline: Celtics Clip Cavs on Tatum's Return
items.summary: The Boston Celtics, with Jayson Tatum back in the lineup scoring 20, defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 109-98.

items.headline: Raptors Rout Mavericks, Barrett Reaches Milestone
items.summary: RJ Barrett notched a season-high 31 points and surpassed 8,000 career points as the Toronto Raptors routed the Dallas Mavericks 122-92.

items.headline: Pelicans Feast on Wizards, Extend Losing Streak
items.summary: Trey Murphy III's 24 points and Saddiq Bey's 23 led the New Orleans Pelicans to a 138-118 win, handing the Washington Wizards their eighth consecutive loss.

items.headline: Henderson's Season-High Powers Blazers Past Pacers
items.summary: Scoot Henderson scored a season-high 28 points to lead the Portland Trail Blazers past the Indiana Pacers 131-111, with Deni Avdija adding 18 points and 8 assists in his return.

items.headline: Wemby and Fox Dominate as Spurs Extend Win Streak
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama scored 29 points and De'Aaron Fox added 20 points and 10 assists as the San Antonio Spurs cruised to a 145-120 victory over the Houston Rockets.

items.headline: Booker's 30 Leads Suns Past Hornets
items.summary: Devin Booker's 30 points helped the Phoenix Suns halt the Charlotte Hornets' road winning streak with a 111-99 victory.

items.headline: SGA Delivers in Thunder Win Over Warriors
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander tallied 25 points to help the Oklahoma City Thunder secure a hard-fought 104-97 win against the Golden State Warriors.

items.headline: Nets Stage Comeback, Beat Pistons in Close Contest
items.summary: The Brooklyn Nets blew past a 23-point deficit to defeat the Detroit Pistons 107-105, with Porter Jr. leading the way with 30 points and 13 rebounds.

items.headline: Banchero Double-Double Cools Down Timberwolves
items.summary: Paolo Banchero recorded 25 points and 15 rebounds as the Orlando Magic cooled down the Minnesota Timberwolves with a 119-92 win, despite Anthony Edwards' 34 points.

items.headline: Westbrook's Triple-Double Leads Kings Over Bulls
items.summary: Russell Westbrook stacked another triple-double to lead the Sacramento Kings to a 126-110 victory over the Chicago Bulls.

items.headline: Doncic Dazzles with 35 Points
items.summary: Luka Doncic led all scorers with 35 points in the Lakers' win over the Knicks.

items.headline: Barrett's Season-High 31 & Career Milestone
items.summary: RJ Barrett scored a season-high 31 points and passed 8,000 career points in the Raptors' victory.

items.headline: Wemby's Dominant 29 Points
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama powered the Spurs with an impressive 29 points against the Rockets.

items.headline: Henderson Hits Season-High 28
items.summary: Scoot Henderson dropped a season-high 28 points in the Trail Blazers' win.

items.headline: Edwards' Explosive 34 Points
items.summary: Anthony Edwards showcased his scoring prowess with 34 points against the Magic.

items.headline: Avdija's Strong Return: 18 Pts, 8 Ast
items.summary: In his return from injury, Deni Avdija contributed 18 points and eight assists for the Blazers.

items.headline: Westbrook Logs Another Triple-Double
items.summary: Russell Westbrook recorded another triple-double in the Kings' decisive win.

items.headline: Banchero's Big Double-Double
items.summary: Paolo Banchero dominated with 25 points and 15 rebounds for the Magic.

items.headline: Porter Jr.'s Double-Double Off the Bench
items.summary: Porter Jr. had a massive impact with 30 points and 13 rebounds coming off the bench for the Nets.

items.headline: Booker Scores 30 in Suns' Victory
items.summary: Devin Booker led the Phoenix Suns with 30 points against the Charlotte Hornets.

items.headline: Anthony Edwards to Hawks Rumors Persist for 2026 Offseason
items.summary: Rumors about Anthony Edwards potentially joining the Atlanta Hawks in the 2026 offseason have gained traction, though a trade is considered unlikely given his long-term contract with Minnesota.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Expected to Dominate Offseason Trade Chatter
items.summary: Giannis Antetokounmpo is still expected to be a major topic in trade discussions once the offseason begins in May, with his future with the Bucks predicated on whether he asks to leave.

items.headline: Kawhi Leonard's Clippers Future Uncertain Amid Contract Scandal
items.summary: Kawhi Leonard's future with the LA Clippers is shrouded in uncertainty, as the NBA's decision on a contract scandal could impact the final year of his deal, potentially leading to a trade.

items.headline: LeBron James Continues to Miss Time with Elbow and Foot Injuries
items.summary: LeBron James missed his second consecutive game for the Los Angeles Lakers due to a bruised left elbow and a left foot injury.

items.headline: OKC Thunder Lead NBA Championship Market, SGA MVP Frontrunner
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder are currently leading the 2026 NBA Champion market, while Shai Gilgeous-Alexander dominates the MVP race.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama Favored for Defensive Player of the Year
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama is heavily favored to win the NBA Defensive Player of the Year award.

items.headline: Today's Marquee Matchups: Nuggets vs. Thunder, Knicks vs. Clippers
items.summary: Tonight's NBA action includes a clash between the Denver Nuggets and Oklahoma City Thunder at 7:30 PM ET, and the New York Knicks taking on the Los Angeles Clippers at 10:00 PM ET.
